strategic_objective: Characterize the stability and discriminative value of class-conditioned variations in backward packet length features under varying conditions.
key_checks: Verify stability of class-conditioned distribution shifts across time windows and network conditions
Compare variance ratios and unique value counts between classes for backward packet length features
Assess risk of overfitting due to dataset-specific artifacts in these features
Evaluate potential for reliable class discrimination based on these structural variations
success_criteria: Demonstrated stability or instability of distributional shifts over relevant conditions
Clear evidence of discriminative power balanced against overfitting risk
Guidance on feature usage for robust classification
router_constraints: preserve feature distribution and variance context
preserve evidence refs e10 to e12
